# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class MarshalCookieStore < ActionDispatch::Session::AbstractStore
  SESSION_COOKIE = "_session".freeze

  def generate_sid
    SecureRandom.hex(32)
  end

  private

  def get_session(env, sid)
    request = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    raw = request.cookie_jar[SESSION_COOKIE]

    data =
      if raw.present?
        begin
          Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(raw))
        rescue StandardError
          {}
        end
      else
        {}
      end

    sid ||= data["_sid"] || generate_sid
    data["_sid"] = sid

    [sid, data]
  end

  def set_session(env, sid, session, options)
    request = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    cookie_jar = request.cookie_jar

    session = session.to_hash
    session["_sid"] = sid || session["_sid"] || generate_sid

    serialized = Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(session))

    cookie_jar[SESSION_COOKIE] = {
      value: serialized,
      httponly: true,
      secure: request.ssl?,
      same_site: :lax,
      path: "/"
    }

    sid
  rescue StandardError
    false
  end

  def destroy_session(env, sid, options)
    request = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    request.cookie_jar.delete(SESSION_COOKIE, path: "/")
    generate_sid
  end
end
